programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: When Tracey decides to organise a meeting of the Prisoners Wives Support Group, Sharon gets more involved than she expected. Comedy, starring Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: Thug on the Tyne - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Rawtons cover is blown, putting Burnside in jeopardy

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: When a man is found dead in a car, Eva and her colleague are called to investigate. Eva becomes suspicious of the deceaseds wife when her story does not ring true. JJ and Archie find common ground when they play football together, and Jimmi tries to hide from Vivien

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Michelle gets roped into judging a cook-off when a father and daughter at loggerheads over how to run their restaurant decide it is the only way to settle their differences regarding the future of the business. Melody tries to make Archie jealous by having lunch with an old flame she runs into

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Connie has received a string of complaints about Will, and puts him under observation for the day. Her concerns seem to be confirmed when he accuses a seriously ill patient of faking her symptoms - only to see her collapse in the wards and her husband threaten legal action. Chrissie urges Tricia to ask Carlos out, the tension rises between Jess and Zubin and Owen is determined to prove how committed he is to Diane and the baby

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: The duo attend a beloved uncles funeral, but June is unaware of the chaos to follow

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Speckled Band
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret are packing for a holiday in Athens - but their preparations are delayed by an unhelpful electricity board representative, as well as an escaped snake. Starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie, with Clive Mantle

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor Meldrew finds himself forced into early retirement, but is determined not to end up on the scrapheap, pouring scorn on the modern world and driving everybody crazy in the process. Richard Wilson stars in the first-ever episode as misery guts Meldrew, with Annette Crosbie as his long-suffering wife. From 1990

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Margaret tries to take Victors mind off his depressing situation by encouraging him to attend a fitness class and read a book on how to cope with old age. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrew household is disrupted by a pesky plumber whose penchant for morbid practical jokes would try the patience of a saint. Victor decides its time to call a neighbourhood watch system after hes mugged and graffiti is spray painted on the side of the Meldrews house. David Renwicks award-winning comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret are trapped in the shed by a swarm of bees - and not unexpectedly where the mishap-prone Meldrews are concerned, that is just the start of their troubles. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor takes up life drawing classes and Margaret is only too happy to see the back of him - until she learns the subject is a nude woman whom he has volunteered to drive home. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Speckled Band
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret are packing for a holiday in Athens - but their preparations are delayed by an unhelpful electricity board representative, as well as an escaped snake. Starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie, with Clive Mantle

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer embarks on a journey across the country, meeting a diverse array of people and encountering exotic wildlife. He begins in Northern Territory, where he discovers how the camel is threatening the fragile desert ecosystem, then heads through wine country to the South Australian city of Port Lincoln to dive with critically endangered southern blue fin tuna. In Western Australia, he joins a 21st-century gold rush and ends his first leg in state capital Perth, where he meets a former binman from Hull who now has a house in the sun, a private pool and his own boat

programme.title: James Mays Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: James Mays Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert

programme.title: James Mays Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the worlds fastest driverless car

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an insight into the most successful animals on the planet. Observing an Arctic fox stalk prey in its -20C environment, he reveals warm-bloodedness as one of the key factors in the mammals global domination. He also studies marsupials in Australia and Central America, offering a unique view inside a platypuss breeding burrow

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough offers an insight into insect-eating animals, including shrews that dive under water, moles that can swim in the Namib Desert, and New Zealand bats that hunt in packs on the ground, walking on their wrists and foraging. Plus, how hedgehogs and armadillos liven up their diet with fruit and birds eggs, while pangolins and giant anteaters survive entirely on a diet of insects

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough uses advanced surveillance technology to observe the feeding habits of herbivores, including elephants forced to undertake epic migrations to ensure a steady diet of the plants they need to survive. He also considers the habits of the sloth, which takes a month to digest each meal, and reveals scientific research suggesting that grazing wildebeest do not follow the rains as originally thought, but detect trails of phosphorus in the grass

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the world of rodents, whose constantly growing incisor teeth are used to chisel through any obstacle and range from squirrels and the English harvest mouse to the large South American capybara. He looks at examples of their dental prowess, showing how agoutis in Panama break open rock-hard nutshells and the skills employed by beavers in Wyoming as they fell massive trees to build dams rivalling feats of human engineering

programme.desc: Victor takes up life drawing classes and Margaret is only too happy to see the back of him - until she learns the subject is a nude woman whom he has volunteered to drive home. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Speckled Band
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret are packing for a holiday in Athens - but their preparations are delayed by an unhelpful electricity board representative, as well as an escaped snake. Starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie, with Clive Mantle

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer embarks on a journey across the country, meeting a diverse array of people and encountering exotic wildlife. He begins in Northern Territory, where he discovers how the camel is threatening the fragile desert ecosystem, then heads through wine country to the South Australian city of Port Lincoln to dive with critically endangered southern blue fin tuna. In Western Australia, he joins a 21st-century gold rush and ends his first leg in state capital Perth, where he meets a former binman from Hull who now has a house in the sun, a private pool and his own boat

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor Meldrew finds himself forced into early retirement, but is determined not to end up on the scrapheap, pouring scorn on the modern world and driving everybody crazy in the process. Richard Wilson stars in the first-ever episode as misery guts Meldrew, with Annette Crosbie as his long-suffering wife. From 1990

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the world of rodents, whose constantly growing incisor teeth are used to chisel through any obstacle and range from squirrels and the English harvest mouse to the large South American capybara. He looks at examples of their dental prowess, showing how agoutis in Panama break open rock-hard nutshells and the skills employed by beavers in Wyoming as they fell massive trees to build dams rivalling feats of human engineering

programme.title: James Mays Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: James Mays Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert

programme.title: James Mays Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the worlds fastest driverless car

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an insight into the most successful animals on the planet. Observing an Arctic fox stalk prey in its -20C environment, he reveals warm-bloodedness as one of the key factors in the mammals global domination. He also studies marsupials in Australia and Central America, offering a unique view inside a platypuss breeding burrow

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough offers an insight into insect-eating animals, including shrews that dive under water, moles that can swim in the Namib Desert, and New Zealand bats that hunt in packs on the ground, walking on their wrists and foraging. Plus, how hedgehogs and armadillos liven up their diet with fruit and birds eggs, while pangolins and giant anteaters survive entirely on a diet of insects

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough uses advanced surveillance technology to observe the feeding habits of herbivores, including elephants forced to undertake epic migrations to ensure a steady diet of the plants they need to survive. He also considers the habits of the sloth, which takes a month to digest each meal, and reveals scientific research suggesting that grazing wildebeest do not follow the rains as originally thought, but detect trails of phosphorus in the grass

programme.title: The Life of Mammals

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Terry acts as Junes defence in court after she is involved in an incident with a corporation dustcart. However, his key witness turns out to be less than reliable in recollecting the facts

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Robin Hoods Bay, the fishing village on the North Yorkshire coast, revealing tales of smuggling intrigue and bravery

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team reveals why the Isle of Harris is the perfect location for road-testing cabriolets, and find out if it is possible to parachute into a moving car travelling at 50mph. Richard Hammond unveils the blueprint for the perfect roadster and Ranulph Fiennes is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets a good soaking when he takes BMWs off-roader, the X3 out for a spin, and Richard Hammond discovers how the Corvette C6 is a break with tradition. Plus, James May discovers the biggest item he can get into the back of a Volvo V50. Comedian Patrick Kielty is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is upset that Trevor has decided to work for a mission project in Africa, and resolves to give him an unforgettable send-off to remind him of the loving family he has in Ireland. Cathys family counselling session descends into chaos, Buster and Dermot land themselves in trouble because of a circus lion, and neighbour Winnie is delighted that her husbands libido has returned. Comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: A secretive phone call from a solicitor to Mrs Brown leaves her children suspecting one of them may be adopted, while Father Quinn returns from rehab and asks Agnes to help out at his annual garden party - although as she proceeds to drive him crazy with a smartphone, his recovery may be short-lived. Meanwhile, Cathy receives a blast from the past, Rory and Dino have a bust-up, and Mark and Betty deliver bad news - they are leaving for Australia right away. Domestic comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The day of the wedding arrives and Bryn is still behaving oddly around Staceys brother. Nessa debates whether to share her big secret and Smithy resolves to give a superb best mans speech - but finds himself overcome with emotion under the spotlight. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Barry and Yvonne tread the light fantastic, and Spike has a humorous conversation with Pinocchio, while Ted attempts to foil Joe Maplins expansion plans. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: The Browns await the fruits of their fraud after faking Grandads death to get a payout, but the insurance company starts to grow suspicious. Agnes struggles to keep him out of sight, as she has promised the money to Dermot and Maria, who are desperate to get a place of their own

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey

programme.title: Dont Wait Up
programme.desc: Tom is expecting to be a father at any minute as Madeline is five days overdue. Toby has his arm in a sling and cant drive, but Felicity comes to the rescue

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom Good decides he has had enough of commuting - but his solution to the problem causes embarrassment among the well-to-do neighbours. First episode of the classic comedy, starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal, Paul Eddington and Penelope Keith

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom and Barbara introduce livestock into Surbiton - an idea that does not go down too well with the neighbours. Comedy, starring Richard Briers and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: The Goods begin to reap their just rewards for the hard work they have put into the garden, although Barbara is too tired and bored to appreciate the benefits. Comedy, starring Richard Briers and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: With the garden under control and the barter system starting to pay off, Tom plans to diversify into pig farming - but underestimates the ferocity of Margos opposition. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal and Penelope Keith

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom draws on every resource in his efforts to practise self-sufficiency, building a generator that runs on animal dung. Jerry takes an interest in the concept till Margo and Barbara have words. Comedy, starring Richard Briers and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom is tempted to earn some extra money, but it is not to pay the bills or replenish his dwindling savings. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal, Penelope Keith and Paul Eddington. First broadcast in 1975

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom finds himself suffering a bad back during harvest time, which leaves Barbara to do all the work on her own - a situation made worse by the terrible weather. Sitcom, starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal, Paul Eddington and Penelope Keith

programme.title: Bottom
programme.desc: The boys join Mrs Linekers Love Bureau hoping to find their perfect love matches. Richies video tells the potential suitors that he is, in fact, a duke and this secures him a date with a countess from Moldavia. Undeterred, he invites her to his place and arranges for Eddie to pretend to be his butler for the evening. Chaotic comedy, written by and starring Adrian Edmondson and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Bottom
programme.desc: Richie and Eddies TV is repossessed, giving them ample opportunity to amuse themselves with a series of violent tit-for-tat assaults thinly disguised as games. Rik Mayall and Adrian Edmondson star

programme.desc: Tom finds himself suffering a bad back during harvest time, which leaves Barbara to do all the work on her own - a situation made worse by the terrible weather. Sitcom, starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal, Paul Eddington and Penelope Keith

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: Agnes gets the wrong end of the stick and fears her relatives are planning to send her to a nursing home. Furious, she sets out to persuade them she is not going senile - unaware it is actually Spartacus the dog who is set to be sent away. Comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine tries to make the supreme sacrifice by giving up chocolate for Lent. Mrs Cropleys also being good, vowing to steer clear of lard and fishpaste pancakes, and even Jim has a bash at self-denial. Meanwhile, Alices unwavering belief in the Dibley Bunny is ridiculed. Easter special from 1996

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldines hopes of romance are fuelled by a visit from Davids handsome brother, while the Hortons return from honeymoon with a souvenir they had not bargained for - Alice is pregnant. The only problem is, she doesnt know it yet. Clerical comedy, starring Dawn French and Emma Chambers. From Christmas Eve 1999

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Alice suggests an unconventional nativity play starring herself and Hugo as Mary and Joseph, and adds an extra touch of authenticity by going into labour mid-performance. The coincidence is so striking that she asks Geraldine whether she has given birth to the Son of God - even though her baby is a girl. Festive episode from 1999, starring Dawn French and Emma Chambers

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Alice and Hugos baby is christened. The godparents are chosen, the Bishop of Mulberry officiates, and David asks Geraldine to marry him. She consents to his proposal - until sense prevails. Comedy, guest starring Sean Bean, with Dawn French, Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet and Emma Chambers

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A summer water shortage presents problems for the locals, especially Alice, whose lack of clean clothes forces her to wear a Wonder Woman outfit instead. The parched residents soon see events turned on their head as the water company offers to resolve the problem by flooding the village to create a reservoir

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom Good decides he has had enough of commuting - but his solution to the problem causes embarrassment among the well-to-do neighbours. First episode of the classic comedy, starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal, Paul Eddington and Penelope Keith

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom and Barbara introduce livestock into Surbiton - an idea that does not go down too well with the neighbours. Comedy, starring Richard Briers and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: The Goods begin to reap their just rewards for the hard work they have put into the garden, although Barbara is too tired and bored to appreciate the benefits. Comedy, starring Richard Briers and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: With the garden under control and the barter system starting to pay off, Tom plans to diversify into pig farming - but underestimates the ferocity of Margos opposition. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal and Penelope Keith

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom draws on every resource in his efforts to practise self-sufficiency, building a generator that runs on animal dung. Jerry takes an interest in the concept till Margo and Barbara have words. Comedy, starring Richard Briers and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom is tempted to earn some extra money, but it is not to pay the bills or replenish his dwindling savings. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal, Penelope Keith and Paul Eddington. First broadcast in 1975

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom finds himself suffering a bad back during harvest time, which leaves Barbara to do all the work on her own - a situation made worse by the terrible weather. Sitcom, starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal, Paul Eddington and Penelope Keith

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is upset that Trevor has decided to work for a mission project in Africa, and resolves to give him an unforgettable send-off to remind him of the loving family he has in Ireland. Cathys family counselling session descends into chaos, Buster and Dermot land themselves in trouble because of a circus lion, and neighbour Winnie is delighted that her husbands libido has returned. Comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: A secretive phone call from a solicitor to Mrs Brown leaves her children suspecting one of them may be adopted, while Father Quinn returns from rehab and asks Agnes to help out at his annual garden party - although as she proceeds to drive him crazy with a smartphone, his recovery may be short-lived. Meanwhile, Cathy receives a blast from the past, Rory and Dino have a bust-up, and Mark and Betty deliver bad news - they are leaving for Australia right away. Domestic comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: First edition of the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedian as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, including a woman frightened of her own shadow, the wife of a cannibal on death row, a couple with a unique approach to parenting, an Irish nurse who will not let patients come between her and her twin passions, and a teenager who would rather be right than happy. From February 2004

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Award-winning comedy sketch series featuring the versatile actress. Bernie the Irish nurse is feeling a little romantic

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The couple announce their engagement, prompting the Shipmans to throw a party in Billericay. Even Bryns decision to use his sat-nav for the journey fails to dampen their spirits, and everything goes swimmingly until the conversation turns to money - and the sweethearts drop a bombshell. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help Lindsey after her mother and extended family leave everything they dont want at her house - causing her to lose her personal style

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Oscar-winning actress at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre, her life away from acting and her relationship with her husband, the late Michael Williams. Louis also accompanies Judi to the Gielgud Theatre in Londons West End, where they discuss the difficulties of the lockdown and how it inadvertently led to her surprise popularity on TikTok

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part three of three. Heatons first suspect provides an alibi for one of the cold case murders and finds himself in the clear as forensic evidence establishes that all three girls were killed in the same manner. Desperate for his freedom, he cooperates with the police by identifying everyone involved in staging the rave events attended by the victims. When the real killer is named, the race is on to find him before he strikes again. James Gaddas guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a cab driver is badly beaten and his takings stolen, an electronic travel card found near the scene provides a lead, linking the crime to a volunteer worker at a support centre on the Jasmine Allen estate. The suspect is questioned and protests her innocence, but when officers approach her again she runs off, spilling a fair amount of cash in the process

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A teenager is found unconscious in the street following a party that went disastrously wrong when a known drug dealer turned up causing trouble. Nate takes an instant shine to a paramedic called to attend to the victim, but later finds himself obliged to arrest her brother for his involvement in the incident. Rachel McDowall guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Nate investigates a knife attack on young girl Aisha, only for the case to take on a personal note when he discovers his new love interest, nurse Maria Joseph, agreed not to report the injury when she treated the wounded girl in hospital. Further evidence reveals that Aisha may be the victim of a stalker - and when she is kidnapped, the race is on to rescue her from a potential killer

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Maxs Polish ancestry proves useful when he investigates an extortion racket in Sun Hills frightened immigrant community. The case brings him into contact with the family of a man he shot dead during his time with the armed response unit. Honeysuckle Weeks guest stars

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben decides to sell his dental practice and take up a hobby. However, he soon finds that his new pastime is even more boring than work. Meanwhile, Susans move up the ladder at the art gallery results in more than one fall. Comedy, starring Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben compounds a past misdemeanour of running over a neighbours dog some years before by doing the same to its owner, Mr Casey, who plays the sympathy card for all it is worth - which turns out to be worth a great deal. Comedy, starring Robert Lindsay and Nickolas Grace

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben tries to get his old band back together, while Kenzos piano playing leaves a lot to be desired. Meanwhile, Michael and Alfies venture into the world of internet dating does not go according to plan. Guest starring Rowland Rivron

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan believes that abstinence makes the heart grow fonder - and while Janey seems to have no choice but to test that theory, Abi views it as a longer-term commitment

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: After struggling to write a loving message inside Susans wedding anniversary card, Ben tags along to a pub quiz with Janey and ends up confronting a cheating opponent. The defiant dentist backs off when the disagreement turns physical, but vows to get into shape to exact his revenge. Comedy, starring Robert Lindsay and Daniela Denby-Ashe

programme.title: Tipping Point

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: As the festive season approaches at Fitzpatrick Referrals, Professor Noel and his team treat three special patients. A one-year-old crossbreed puppy rescued by a charity from Romania is in need of complex surgery, with no guarantee of success. An eight-year-old Dalmatian needs work on its spinal cord, and a Labrador from Switzerland is having trouble walking. Noels investigations reveal problems with both shoulder and elbow, an unusual combination that requires dramatic intervention

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats Koa, a six-month-old Bouvier des Flandres who is suffering from a badly deformed back leg, causing huge pain. Patricia brings her beloved 11-year-old Rhodesian ridgeback, Kwanda, to Fitzpatricks Guildford hospital with an eye infection that she fears may be the sign of something more sinister. Dr Laurent Findji confirms it is bad news - a malignant tumour

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats an 11-month-old dog who has a severely dislocated hip which Noel suspects is the result of trauma experienced at the tender age of only two months. Elsewhere, overweight Labradoodle Fletcher is brought into the veterinary practice all the way from Scotland after previous surgery to fix a bad ankle fracture has failed, and a tiny hedgehog needs treatment on his broken hind legs

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel suspects that an 18-month-old Australian Shepherd dog may be suffering from nerve compression, and an 11-month-old pug shows signs of neurological damage. It transpires that she has bone malformation that is squashing her spinal cord. Elsewhere, there is an emergency admission when a Jack Russell puppy called Bella is rushed in with a crushed pelvis after a freak accident

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog called Dave has a spinal deformity, and Noel discovers that not only does he have a genetic malformation of his sacral and lumbar vertebrae, but also excess bone in the pathways where the nerves travel to his legs. Meanwhile, a six-year-old terrier has lost a paw after being attacked by another dog, and the only options appear to be replacing the paw with a bionic implant or amputating the entire limb

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Portsmouth, the paramedics treat a woman who was kicked by a horse and a baby with breathing problems, while in Oxford, crews attend an elderly priest with abdominal pain

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Oxford paramedics are called to a woman who sustained a broken leg and horrific facial injuries, while in Portsmouth, a two-year-old with breathing problems needs a nebuliser

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: A seven-year-old has an operation to straighten her squint and an ex-Army man, who served in Afghanistan and Iraq, has a procedure to get rid of constant back pain

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Anne needs an operation to remove a painful bony lump from her foot and mother-of-four Laura decides to have a sterilisation after suffering from post-natal depression

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: First edition of the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedian as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, including a woman frightened of her own shadow, the wife of a cannibal on death row, a couple with a unique approach to parenting, an Irish nurse who will not let patients come between her and her twin passions, and a teenager who would rather be right than happy. From February 2004

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Award-winning comedy sketch series featuring the versatile actress. Bernie the Irish nurse is feeling a little romantic

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A Nigerian mum who demands respect from her kids swaps places with an overwhelmed mum who gets little help from her husband in raising their spoiled children

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: Differently shaped worlds collide when a rule-obsessed school teacher swaps places with a free-spirited, homeschooling mum who believes that the Earth is flat

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Suspected of being in a romantic relationship, Lo and Kayla are still being kept apart by officers - and Lo has a difficult decision to make. When jail veterans Jackie and Kourtney are moved, they bring with them all the latest news. Detox over, heroin addict Natasha is moved into the jails general population. Used to living with her mother, how she will cope when thrown into an eight-person tank remains to be seen

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help Godfrey face his grief by helping him clean a basement full of his deceased parents things that he doesnt want to get rid of

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.desc: In Dudley, a man collapses while playing bingo two days before his wedding, while in Stoke, crews are called to help an elderly man who has had an unexplained fall.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Oscar-winning actress at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre, her life away from acting and her relationship with her husband, the late Michael Williams. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part three of three. Heatons first suspect provides an alibi for one of the cold case murders and finds himself in the clear as forensic evidence establishes that all three girls were killed in the same manner. Desperate for his freedom, he cooperates with the police by identifying everyone involved in staging the rave events attended by the victims. When the real killer is named, the race is on to find him before he strikes again. James Gaddas guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a cab driver is badly beaten and his takings stolen, an electronic travel card found near the scene provides a lead, linking the crime to a volunteer worker at a support centre on the Jasmine Allen estate. The suspect is questioned and protests her innocence, but when officers approach her again she runs off, spilling a fair amount of cash in the process

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A teenager is found unconscious in the street following a party that went disastrously wrong when a known drug dealer turned up causing trouble. programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: TV presenters Carol Vorderman and Joe Swash and Emmerdales Mark Charnock (Marlon Dingle) answer questions to win turns on the arcade-style token-pushing machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of scooping the £20,000 prize for their chosen charity. Ben Shephard hosts

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A fully restored Class 47 diesel engine is transported from its home at Crewe Heritage Centre in Cheshire to the Ecclesbourne Valley Railway in Derbyshire

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks back in his old stomping ground of Luton, to meet an old schoolmate who is saying goodbye to his 1912 De Dion Bouton

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks off to West Yorkshire to extract a 1987 Peugeot 205 GTI from a garage in a narrow lane. Its one of the more prized 1.9s and has been stored safely away for many years

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcocks films and Graham Greenes books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos 1930s Bradshaws-inspired railway tour continues in north Wales. In Colwyn Bay, Michael hears how an army of civil servants from the Ministries of Food and Agriculture in London invaded the quiet seaside town, before visiting an amusement park in the holiday resort of Rhyl. Crossing the Menai Strait by the Britannia Bridge, Michael arrives on Anglesey and the elegant seat of the Marquesses of the island

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his 1936 Bradshaws-inspired railway tour of north Wales. He begins in the coastal city of Bangor, where he hears how, when war broke out in September 1939, 2,000 children from the cities of northern England were evacuated by train to north Wales. Heading inland alongside the River Conwy, Michael reaches Tal y Cafn, before investigating the worst dam disaster in Welsh history in Dolgarrog. Finally in Betws y Coed, he attempts to climb one of Snowdonias lesser peaks

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In the Welsh mountains of Snowdonia, Michael Portillo reaches an abandoned mine with an art-related history. He also explores the harbour town of Porthmadog, and the fantasy village Portmeirion, crafted by architect Bertram Clough Williams-Ellis, and made famous by The Prisoner TV series

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In the elegant coastal town of Aberystwyth, Michael Portillo visits the National Library of Wales, set high on a hill with views over Cardigan Bay. His tour of North Wales draws to a close in style at the birthplace of the nations first classical music festival, Gregynog Hall, near Newtown

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits a vast airplane salvage yard. With some unusual buys in the van, he has his work cut out to re-invent them into design pieces he hopes will fly off the shelf

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits a grand Victorian school where hes given access to huge cellars full of old treasures. Using what he finds, he creates two formidable pieces of furniture

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits an impressive helicopter museum. He buys a helicopter exhaust unit and a set of massive rotor blades and transforms them into remarkable pieces of furniture

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis channels his love of cars to transform a vintage Bentley engine part into a must-have light. He also turns an old icebox into a shining contemporary cabinet

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: The end of the war sees Rene, Edith and the rest of the gang welcoming in the Allied troops, and they soon find that the invasion party includes a couple of old friends

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Crafty con Fletcher helps new inmate Godber settle in by giving him a few lessons on coping with prison life. First episode of the classic comedy, originally broadcast in 1974, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but he experiences engine issues and a leaky boat along the way

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming battles his way through blanket weed as he travels along the Shropshire Union Canal and discovers industrial secrets in Audlem, Cheshire

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming gets stuck in the mud in Woodseaves Cutting, and explores the charming canal-side village of Kinver in Staffordshire. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming navigates the River Severn, and takes an unexpected bath as he tumbles at the Tardebigge lock flight in Worcestershire. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: The workshop gets a new wall and floor while Sam Lovegrove mends Doms vintage motorbike for a cliff top trial

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: The adventurer sets off on a journey through the continent, beginning in the remote and little-visited north-east. In Venezuela, Simon meets some of the thousands of migrants fleeing the economic collapse of their country, while he learns that neighbouring Guyana could be on the verge of its own oil boom. He also meets a gun-toting former warlord who is now one of Surinames most successful businessmen, and ends his journey in French Guiana, where he goes to the launch site of the European Space Agency

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: Simon starts his travels through Brazil in one of the remotest regions of the Amazon with the Waiapi people, who cling on to their traditional way of life, which is under threat from logging and mining interests. In Manaus, the city at the heart of the Amazon, he visits a neglected indigenous neighbourhood and meets a nurse who was the only source of healthcare during the pandemic. Simon ends this leg of his journey on the west coast in the city of Rio de Janeiro, where the government has built a hi-tech control room to monitor all parts of the city for potential disasters such as floods

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of flight, from the first glider to the modern jet suit, and discovers how Britain contributed to the moon landing

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up some very special working vehicles. Charlie feels the pull of a gargantuan Hummer and Paul extracts a vintage tractor from a Yorkshire barn

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A beautiful looking sports car has been fooling the punters and intriguing Derek. Its a Challenger E-Type - a fibreglass replica created from an E-Type mould

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Documentary following the exploits of a family-run crew of heavy-haulage specialists as they transport the heaviest, longest and most precious locomotives around the world. Terry and Jason have been called in to move legendary steam locomotive Britannia. The 68-year-old vehicle has undergone a major restoration in Crewe and is scheduled to start running at the Severn Valley Railway

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Theres Hollywood glamour in the air as Derek heads out to pick up a Rolls-Royce that once belonged to Fred Astaire

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek is reunited with the vehicle he took his driving test in - a 1957 Ford 100E Estate. Dave is also on the trail for an Escort, this time a 1970s Mk1 twin cam

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks search for the unusual lands him a 1947 Lea-Francis thats half made of wood. Paul heads for the countryside to pick up a Morris one tonne truck

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave and Jack head to East Yorkshire to pick up a dusty Alfa Romeo and a 1930s Jowett. But Daves more taken with a Carry On-style, bum-wobbling exercise machine

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo takes a trip in a Second World War Spitfire above Biggin Hill aerodrome, and takes the train to East Grinstead on the trail of Winnie the Pooh. He also visits Plaw Hatch Farm, 200 acres of community-owned farmland that operates biodynamically according to principles first laid out in the 1920s

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Hassocks and makes his way to the beautiful Sussex village of Ditchling, where a Roman Catholic community of artists once made their home. Next, he visits the seaside town of Bexhill-on-Sea, before the Kent and East Sussex heritage railway conveys Michael from Bodiam Castle through the scenic Rother Valley to Tenterden, and finally arrives in the village of Benenden

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo follows his 1930s Bradshaws guide to the unspoilt East Sussex port of Rye, where he learns about the loss of a generation of lifeboatmen in 1928. On the beaches at Rye, Michael explores one of 28,000 pillboxes constructed around the British coastline during the Second World War, before boarding the Romney, Hythe and Dymchurch railway to Dungeness

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From the Kent Cinque Port of Deal, Michael Portillo heads to Walmer Castle, home during the 1920s to Lord Beauchamp, whose openly homosexual lifestyle led to his exile from the country. And in Margate, Michael discovers the connection to TS Eliot

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Acclaimed chronicle of the Second World War, beginning with an account of Hitlers ascent to power at a time when the Germans were stricken by humiliating military defeat, runaway inflation and economic depression. The Nazi leaders promises of regained national pride and prosperity struck a chord with millions of ordinary citizens, and he was elected on a tidal wave of popular support. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The months following prime minister Neville Chamberlains declaration of war against Germany, in which the only casualties on the home front were those who had to endure the blackouts. Elsewhere in Europe, the Nazi war machine cut a swathe of destruction. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but he experiences engine issues and a leaky boat along the way

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming battles his way through blanket weed as he travels along the Shropshire Union Canal and discovers industrial secrets in Audlem, Cheshire

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming gets stuck in the mud in Woodseaves Cutting, and explores the charming canal-side village of Kinver in Staffordshire. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming navigates the River Severn, and takes an unexpected bath as he tumbles at the Tardebigge lock flight in Worcestershire. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of America
programme.desc: The actor arrives in New York to explore Manhattan Island, and its Empire State Building, from where he can see the citys 500 miles of coastline and more than 40 islands. He later takes the ferry to Ellis Island, which opened in 1892 as an immigration centre for those arriving in the US by sea. Following a trip to Coney Island, home to the iconic amusement park, he explores Marthas Vineyard more than 200 miles away, where Jaws was made. Martin ends his trip in Maine, Americas most easterly state, with hundreds of islands off its coast

programme.title: Joanna and Jennifer: Absolutely Champers
programme.desc: In Absolutely Fabulous, Patsy and Edina were rarely seen without a glass of champagne in their hands. And it turns out their real-life counterparts, comedy collaborators and great friends Joanna Lumley and Jennifer Saunders, also like nothing more than getting together to share a glass of something chilled and bubbly. Now the pair head across the channel to explore the Champagne region of France and find out how the tipple is made. Along the way, they also quiz each other about their lives, revealing previously unheard stories and anecdotes

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter explores the country that lies at the crossroads between Europe and Asia, and discovers the dramatic changes it is undergoing. The first leg of his journey begins in Istanbul, from which he travels along the Aegean coast to the hostile border with Syria. He meets refugees trying to start a new life and a billionaire taking advantage of a property boom. He moves on into the Taurus Mountains and the Black Sea Coast, and visits a community trying to keep an ancient language alive

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the second half of the journey, Simon travels along the Black Sea Coast to the capital of Ankara and the Northern Highlands, witnessing first hand the aftermath of fighting in Kurdish areas and meeting a conservationist attempting to preserve the regions brown bears. He then returns to Istanbul, where he takes part in a popular TV series set in the palace of the Ottoman Sultans

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his epic journey across the north of England, narrowboater Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop. His destination is Ripon Basin at the outer reaches of Britains inland waterways

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a fresh set of adventures along the waterways of the north of England and the Midlands. He is forced to make a running repair to his narrowboat the Naughty Lass while on the Tinsley lock flight in South Yorkshire. He then cruises beneath Conisbroughs magnificent viaduct and must wait for an unusual sliding railway bridge to open on the Stainforth and Keadby Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Trent and is invited to join a night-time illuminated boat parade. However, his journey is hampered by a damaged wall in Worksop and a water shortage on the Chesterfield Canal which threatens his voyage

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Autumnal fog and an overheating engine prove to be problems for narrowboater Robbie Cumming. As he navigates a Roman waterway, it proves to be a journey of many challenges as he runs aground and breaks down in Nottingham

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: The workshop gets a new wall and floor while Sam Lovegrove mends Doms vintage motorbike for a cliff top trial

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: First episode of the classic comedy sequel. Bob travels down to London in his efforts to find a suitable wedding present, where fate sees him reunited with Terry, returning home after a gruelling spell in the Army. Starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam. From 1973

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Terry arrives home to find his parents on holiday, the fridge empty, the house cold and Thelma her usual unhelpful self. Luckily Bob is on hand with a suggestion. Comedy, starring James Bolam and Rodney Bewes, with Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob has doubts about his marriage when he realises all his peers - even the vicar - seem to be getting divorced. Classic comedy, starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up some very special working vehicles. Charlie feels the pull of a gargantuan Hummer and Paul extracts a vintage tractor from a Yorkshire barn

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team think they can smell profit by choosing one of the simplest cars ever built, the VW Beetle

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Austin Healeys are classic British vehicles that are difficult to tame. Paul and Charlie have the rare chance to pick up a trio of these iconic cars in varying conditions

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks got his work cut out collecting a huge haul that includes two Bentleys, a Daimler and a traditional Bowtop caravan

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave has to extricate a VW Golf from a garden that has grown up around it

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Herne Bay to Leeds Castle, Kent
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the Kent seaside resort of Herne Bay, where he learns about a pioneering aviatrix who began her working life as a typist in Hull. In the seaside town of Whitstable, he tucks into its famous staple, oysters. Michael later crosses the North Downs to Lenham, and visits Leeds Castle

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels in Greater London, beginning on its south-eastern fringe in the village of Chislehurst and ending up in Kennington to learn about the childhood of Charlie Chaplin

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through the capital in the heart of Londons East End. First, Michael explores Hackney Wick, where he hears about The Daily Worker newspapers origins, before heading to Londons West End to hunt down Londons oldest surviving Indian restaurant, Veeraswamy. Here, he tries his hand at making chapatis, under the watchful eye of the restaurants master chef

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through London in Hampstead, where he learns more about the founder of psychoanalysis, Sigmund Freud, who fled the Austrian capital of Vienna during the Second World War and made his way London. Then, Michael pays a visit to the famous Abbey Road Studios, before making his way to Finsbury and touring the innovative and sculptural housing development, Bevin Court

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up a quartet of tasty vehicles from a farm in Shropshire. Among them a 1937 Austin Seven van and a Triumph TR2 from the mid-50s

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul and his son Charlie head to Leicestershire to collect a vintage Albion truck. Steeped in nostalgia, both Paul and Charlie fall for the Albions charms

programme.desc: Robbie Cumming gets stuck in the mud in Woodseaves Cutting, and explores the charming canal-side village of Kinver in Staffordshire. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming navigates the River Severn, and takes an unexpected bath as he tumbles at the Tardebigge lock flight in Worcestershire. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of America
programme.desc: The actor arrives in New York to explore Manhattan Island, and its Empire State Building, from where he can see the citys 500 miles of coastline and more than 40 islands. He later takes the ferry to Ellis Island, which opened in 1892 as an immigration centre for those arriving in the US by sea. Following a trip to Coney Island, home to the iconic amusement park, he explores Marthas Vineyard more than 200 miles away, where Jaws was made. Martin ends his trip in Maine, Americas most easterly state, with hundreds of islands off its coast

programme.title: Joanna and Jennifer: Absolutely Champers
programme.desc: In Absolutely Fabulous, Patsy and Edina were rarely seen without a glass of champagne in their hands. And it turns out their real-life counterparts, comedy collaborators and great friends Joanna Lumley and Jennifer Saunders, also like nothing more than getting together to share a glass of something chilled and bubbly. Now the pair head across the channel to explore the Champagne region of France and find out how the tipple is made. Along the way, they also quiz each other about their lives, revealing previously unheard stories and anecdotes

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter explores the country that lies at the crossroads between Europe and Asia, and discovers the dramatic changes it is undergoing. The first leg of his journey begins in Istanbul, from which he travels along the Aegean coast to the hostile border with Syria. He meets refugees trying to start a new life and a billionaire taking advantage of a property boom. He moves on into the Taurus Mountains and the Black Sea Coast, and visits a community trying to keep an ancient language alive

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the second half of the journey, Simon travels along the Black Sea Coast to the capital of Ankara and the Northern Highlands, witnessing first hand the aftermath of fighting in Kurdish areas and meeting a conservationist attempting to preserve the regions brown bears. He then returns to Istanbul, where he takes part in a popular TV series set in the palace of the Ottoman Sultans

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his epic journey across the north of England, narrowboater Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop. His destination is Ripon Basin at the outer reaches of Britains inland waterways

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a fresh set of adventures along the waterways of the north of England and the Midlands. He is forced to make a running repair to his narrowboat the Naughty Lass while on the Tinsley lock flight in South Yorkshire. He then cruises beneath Conisbroughs magnificent viaduct and must wait for an unusual sliding railway bridge to open on the Stainforth and Keadby Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Trent and is invited to join a night-time illuminated boat parade. However, his journey is hampered by a damaged wall in Worksop and a water shortage on the Chesterfield Canal which threatens his voyage

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Autumnal fog and an overheating engine prove to be problems for narrowboater Robbie Cumming. As he navigates a Roman waterway, it proves to be a journey of many challenges as he runs aground and breaks down in Nottingham

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: The workshop gets a new wall and floor while Sam Lovegrove mends Doms vintage motorbike for a cliff top trial

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: First episode of the classic comedy sequel. Bob travels down to London in his efforts to find a suitable wedding present, where fate sees him reunited with Terry, returning home after a gruelling spell in the Army. Starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam. From 1973

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Terry arrives home to find his parents on holiday, the fridge empty, the house cold and Thelma her usual unhelpful self. Luckily Bob is on hand with a suggestion. Comedy, starring James Bolam and Rodney Bewes, with Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob has doubts about his marriage when he realises all his peers - even the vicar - seem to be getting divorced. Classic comedy, starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up some very special working vehicles. Charlie feels the pull of a gargantuan Hummer and Paul extracts a vintage tractor from a Yorkshire barn

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Theres Hollywood glamour in the air as Derek heads out to pick up a Rolls-Royce that once belonged to Fred Astaire

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up a quartet of tasty vehicles from a farm in Shropshire. Among them a 1937 Austin Seven van and a Triumph TR2 from the mid-50s

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks got his work cut out collecting a huge haul that includes two Bentleys, a Daimler and a traditional Bowtop caravan

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave has to extricate a VW Golf from a garden that has grown up around it

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Herne Bay to Leeds Castle, Kent
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the Kent seaside resort of Herne Bay, where he learns about a pioneering aviatrix who began her working life as a typist in Hull. In the seaside town of Whitstable, he tucks into its famous staple, oysters. Michael later crosses the North Downs to Lenham, and visits Leeds Castle

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels in Greater London, beginning on its south-eastern fringe in the village of Chislehurst and ending up in Kennington to learn about the childhood of Charlie Chaplin

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through the capital in the heart of Londons East End. First, Michael explores Hackney Wick, where he hears about The Daily Worker newspapers origins, before heading to Londons West End to hunt down Londons oldest surviving Indian restaurant, Veeraswamy. Here, he tries his hand at making chapatis, under the watchful eye of the restaurants master chef

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through London in Hampstead, where he learns more about the founder of psychoanalysis, Sigmund Freud, who fled the Austrian capital of Vienna during the Second World War and made his way London. Then, Michael pays a visit to the famous Abbey Road Studios, before making his way to Finsbury and touring the innovative and sculptural housing development, Bevin Court

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up a quartet of tasty vehicles from a farm in Shropshire. Among them a 1937 Austin Seven van and a Triumph TR2 from the mid-50s

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul and his son Charlie head to Leicestershire to collect a vintage Albion truck. Steeped in nostalgia, both Paul and Charlie fall for the Albions charms

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchills forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, Americas reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry presents a documentary looking at the impact of the seasons on wildlife, with first edition examining winter in the northern hemisphere and summer in the southern. On Norwegian archipelago Svalbards frozen sea-ice, a polar bear mother prepares her cub for a life alone, while Marion Island in the Indian Ocean between South Africa and Antarctica becomes home to a million penguins as breeding season starts

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets enthusiastic collie greyhound cross Bud, who holds the record as Batterseas most unwanted resident, having been at the kennels for more than two years. He has boundless energy and needs an owner to match, so Paul enters him into a doggie fun run, hoping he will catch the eye of the athletic pet-lovers. He also helps spaniel Willow, who was found tied to a tree outside Batterseas Old Windsor site in a dreadful condition, and acts as masseur to Jenson the staffie, who suffered accidental scarring that has left his back dry and itchy

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul is in puppy heaven when he meets a litter of newborn Staffie cross pups - but things turn serious when three of them fall sick and are rushed into intensive care. The presenter also gets to grips with feisty collie pup Tilly, assisted by a 13-year-old girl visiting Battersea with the Make-a-Wish foundation, and helps doddery 16-year-old Tiny, a Yorkie whose arthritis has the medical team worried

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Carolines father Henry suggests turning off her life-support machine, presenting Dr Ormerod with a dilemma. Goodwin puts his career on the line by allowing a holistic therapist to treat a back injury and student nurse Samantha Beaumont is less than impressed by a date with theatre technician Alun Morris. Medical drama, starring Robert Daws, Michael Jayston and Amy Robbins

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Goodwins medical skills are brought into question when he wrongly accuses a cafe of making a local jazz band ill and a dinner party turns murderous for Ormerod and Weatherill when their host reveals his wife is trying to kill him. The Health Authority announce plans to reorganise management at the Royal, leaving Mr Harper less than keen to reapply for his job, so Ken and Alun hit upon a plan to convince him otherwise

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager sits down with Tommy Martin. In the first part, he discusses his playing days, including the glory years of Irelands World Cup campaigns

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets Max the setter cross, who is struggling to make any friends - either human or canine - because he is Batterseas smelliest dog. Can the vets get to the bottom of his off-putting body odour? The programme also features Ethel the cavachon, who is finding life in kennels rather overwhelming, and Milo the one-year-old Labrador, who likes nothing more than charging around after tennis balls. However, his knee ligament is damaged and unless treated, meaning he has to undergo major surgery

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul pays his final visit of the series to Battersea, where first up is old English sheepdog puppy Dudley, who is profoundly deaf, which is making training tricky. Paul attempts to teach him some sign-language - but the pooch is more interested in playing. Ten-year-old English bull terrier Beryl is also brought in, although the vet is concerned by a number of growth on her body, and Paul catches up with large akita Igloo, who he met in last years Christmas special and who still hasnt found a home after more than 250 days

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Brigid arrives at a decision and Lucy Klein is disturbed by the unscheduled reappearance of a figure from the past. Mr Harper rests on his laurels, certain the future is assured, while Ormerod and Weatherill reveal they are about to end their relationship - only to find the news is already common knowledge. Drama, starring Linda Armstrong, Polly Maberly and Robert Daws

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Goodwin comes to the aid of a man brought in with a leg injury which his friend claims was sustained in an agricultural accident, but it soon emerges he is actually suffering a gunshot wound - prompting his panic-stricken acquaintance to pull a revolver on the hospital staff. Meanwhile, Ormerod helps fossil collectors hurt in a landslide, Matron tries to convince Sister Brigid to return to work and student nurse Samantha Beaumont is less than impressed at the offer of a date with Alun Morris. Medical drama, starring Paul Fox, Tony Pitts, Denis Lill, Robert Daws and Wendy Craig

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
